summary: Rough recording with bass distortion but a stellar second set – Pigpen shines, Empty Pages and Saint Stephen stand out.
review: Fans rave about the energy of this ’71 outdoor show, especially Pigpen’s performances, the heartbreaking “Empty Pages,” and a tight, pop‑groove take on “Saint Stephen.” The soundboard is uneven – bass muddies in places and the mix drops in and out – yet the second set sounds crisp enough to make the concert worth a listen.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical praise across most reviews (average 4.4★) outweighs the single noted recording flaw; show quality is high, recording quality fair.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Bass heavily distorted and occasional drop‑outs (“goes in and out”), but later parts of the set are clearer and more balanced; no explicit soundboard/audience label.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set feels fresh and vibrant with many new, unreleased tunes; Pigpen dominates the first half while the second half builds into a powerful emotional climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Interesting rhythm patterns on “Truckin’” and a noticeable punch‑up of T’s at phrase ends.
band_member_comments.Phil: "Phil bombs like elephants walk" – heavy, thundering bass noted throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir takes lead vocal on a Rider lyric (“wish I was a headlight…”) – a rare Bob‑lead moment.
band_member_comments.Keys: Subtle, polished keyboard textures under Bobby McGee; occasional “Pig” keys in the background.
band_member_comments.Drums: A 14‑second drum excerpt split off as its own track on “The Other One”; tambourine highlighted on “Saint Stephen.”
